channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - gameinformer.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for gameinformer.com

item.title: Stranger Than Heaven Will Take Place Across Five Time Periods, Deep-Dive Presentation Arriving This May
item.content:encoded:
Like a Dragon/Yakuza developer RGG Studio has released a new trailer for its upcoming game, Stranger Than Heaven, which reveals that its narrative will unfold across five different time periods. Plus, RGG has announced that a deep-dive presentation for the game is coming in May. 

Stranger Than Heaven was first announced under the working title Project Century at The Game Awards 2024, revealing a jazz-infused action game set across different historical periods. A year later, at The Game Awards 2025, RGG released a new trailer for the game, revealing the official "Stranger Than Heaven" name in the process. We still don't know when to expect Stranger Than Heaven, but today's new trailer, which aired during an Xbox Partner Preview showcase, reveals that its story spans different eras of history: 1915, 1929, 1943, 1951, and 1965. 

Check it out for yourself in the latest Stranger Than Heaven trailer below: 

Alongside this new trailer, Xbox and RGG have revealed they will hold an "Xbox Presents: A Special Look At Stranger Than Heaven" presentation on May 6, promising a deep-dive "into the setting, story, characters, and much more." 

For more details about the game, head to this Xbox Wire blog post. 

Are you excited by Stranger Than Heaven's latest showing? Let us know in the comments below!



https://gameinformer.com/xbox-partner-preview/2026/03/26/stranger-than-heaven-will-take-place-across-five-time-periods-deep
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Dead By Daylight Devs Announce Serious Sam: Shatterverse, A Multiverse, Multiplayer Roguelite Shooter
item.content:encoded:
Serious Sam is making his return, and this time, he's not alone. Revealed during today's Xbox Partner Preview, Serious Sam: Shatterverse is a Spiderverse-inspired take on the first-person shooter, bringing Sams from several dimensions into the same adventure. You can check out the reveal trailer below.

Shatterverse features five playable Sams, each with distinct art styles. Since the game is multiplayer, you and up to four friends can play at once, each assuming the role of a different Sam. Classic weapons will return, but they'll exist alongside new "all-new toys" from the Shatterverse. While previous games in the series were helmed by Croatian studio Croteam, Shatterverse is being developed by Dead by Daylight developer Behaviour Interactive, with Devolver Digital returning to publish. 

“Our main goal was to respect Serious Sam's rich legacy while presenting it in a fresh, contemporary way unlike anything seen before in the series,” Behaviour Interactive’s Nic Duchesne wrote in a press release. “Working with Devolver has been an incredible, enriching experience for our team. The folks there are real renegades of the video game publishing world: they always put the game first and aren’t afraid to carve their own path.”

While the game was announced during the Xbox Partner Preview, it's not exclusive to the platform – Serious Sam: Shatterverse launches on PlayStation 5, Xbox Series X/S, and PC later this year.



https://gameinformer.com/xbox-partner-preview/2026/03/26/dead-by-daylight-devs-announce-serious-sam-shatterverse-a
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Hunter: The Reckoning – Deathwish Is A First-Person Action Game Set In The 'World Of Darkness' Universe Arriving Summer 2027
item.content:encoded:
Hunter: The Reckoning – Deathwish is a first-person action game set in the World of Darkness universe, which also includes franchises like Vampire: The Masquerade. Microsoft opened today's Xbox Partner Preview with this announcement, revealing Deathwish will hit Xbox Series X/S, Xbox on PC, and Xbox Cloud in the Summer of 2027. 

This first trailer follows a team of hunters out to take down a dangerous vampire masquerading (wink wink) as a police officer while also teasing some of the other locations you'll explore in the game. If you've been gaming for a minute, you might recognize Hunter: The Reckoning, as it was a hack-and-slash/third-person shooter that launched on the original Xbox in 2002 and the Nintendo GameCube the following year. 

Check out the Hunter: The Reckoning – Deathwish reveal trailer for yourself below: 

You can read more about the game at the Xbox Wire blog post here. 

Deathwish follows last year's Vampire: The Masquerade – Bloodlines 2, the most recent game in the World of Darkness universe, and though they occupy the same world, it remains unclear if they're connected at all. You can read Game Informer's Vampire: The Masquerade – Bloodlines 2 review here, and then check out our Vampire: The Masquerade – Bloodlines 2 hub for more behind-the-scenes information about the World of Darkness from our cover story last year. 

Did you play the original Hunter: The Reckoning? Let us know in the comments below!



https://gameinformer.com/xbox-partner-preview/2026/03/26/hunter-the-reckoning-deathwish-is-a-first-person-action-game-set-in
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
